data.title: Migrate browser automation to @playwright/cli for sub-agent reliability
data.description:
## Context

DSO's design pipeline uses Playwright for live application review during wireframe creation (design-wireframe Phase 2). Currently this runs via temporary Bash scripts (`npx playwright`), which hits the ~73s Bash tool timeout for multi-step browser sessions. The alternative — Playwright MCP — is unreliable in custom sub-agents: multiple open issues (anthropics/claude-code#13605, #13898, #21560) document that plugin-defined sub-agents cannot access MCP tools and instead hallucinate results. Microsoft released `@playwright/cli` as a token-efficient alternative (~4x fewer tokens than MCP) designed for coding agents with filesystem access — short discrete commands (snapshot, click) that each complete under the timeout, with results saved to disk.

## Success Criteria

1. All DSO browser automation uses `@playwright/cli` commands via Bash instead of temporary Playwright scripts or MCP tool calls
2. Browser interactions complete within the ~73s Bash tool timeout (each command is discrete, not a multi-step script)
3. The `dso:playwright-debug` skill uses `@playwright/cli` for its browser interaction tier
4. Sub-agents dispatched via the Agent tool can perform browser automation without MCP access

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Adopt `@playwright/cli` as the browser automation interface across DSO. Replace temporary multi-step Playwright scripts with discrete CLI commands (snapshot, click, fill, etc.) that save results to disk. Update design-wireframe's Phase 2 application review and playwright-debug's browser interaction tier to use the CLI pattern.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered — researched Playwright MCP access in sub-agents; found @playwright/cli as alternative; confirmed MCP inheritance bugs in custom sub-agents
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): not triggered
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
